chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C.P. No.D-4535 of 2013 --------------------------------------------------------------------- Date Order with signature of Judge --------------------------------------------------------------------- Present Mr. Justice Muhammad Ali Mazhar Mr. Justice Arshad Hussain Khan Doctor Muhammad Ismail Bhutto.................................Petitioner V E R S U S The Province of Sindh & others...............................Respondents Date of hearing: 06.10.2016 Mr. Abdul Salam Memon, Advocate for Petitioner. Mr. M. Arshad Khan Tanoli, Advocate for Respondents Nos. 8, 11 and 12. R. Iqbal Khurram, Advocate for Respondents Nos.5, 7 and 10. Mr. Munir-ur-Rehman, Advocate for Respondent No.3. Ch. Muhammad Ashraf Khan, Advocate for Respondents Nos.8 and 9. Mr. Abdul Jalil Zubedi, A.A.G. Respondent No.8 Omperkash, (R) Ex.P.D (MDA) - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - Muhammad Ali Mazhar, J: All learned counsel mutually state that vide Notification dated 21.09.2016 issued by Secretary to Government of Sindh, Local Government & Housing Town Planning Department, 98 persons have been repatriated and relieved to join their parent departments with immediate effect in view of the order of hon’ble Supreme Court of Pakistan passed in C.P.No.108-K/2014 on CMA No.243/2016. The petitioner has challenged through this petition the promotion of some deputationists and since by way of latest notification which we have mentioned above 98 persons have already been relieved and repatriated to their parent departments, therefore, this petition has served its purpose. At this juncture, learned counsel for respondents Nos.8, 11 and 12 submits that before issuing this Notification respondents No.8 and 12 have already been retired.

The petitioner has challenged through this petition the promotion of some deputationists and since by way of latest notification which we have mentioned above 98 persons have already been relieved and repatriated to their parent departments, therefore, this petition has served its purpose. At this juncture, learned counsel for respondents Nos.8, 11 and 12 submits that before issuing this Notification respondents No.8 and 12 have already been retired. This statement is taken on record. The petition is disposed of alongwith pending application. Judge Judge Asif
